title: Meaty and Peppery Irish Sea Bass Bake
description: A peppery and crunchy Irish dish featuring sea bass that can be prepared in advance. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Korean Chili Flakes (1 tsp)
ingredients.notes: Add an extra kick of spice

ingredients.name: Garlic (2 cloves)
ingredients.notes: Mince before adding to the mixture

ingredients.name: Olive Oil (for greasing the baking sheet)

instructions: Season the sea bass fillets with coarse black pepper and a pinch of salt.
In a large mixing bowl, combine softened unsalted butter, plain Greek yogurt, minced garlic, and Korean chili flakes. Mix until smooth and creamy.
Grease a 9x13 inch baking sheet with olive oil and set aside.
In a separate pan, saut7e the sliced leeks and garlic until softened and fragrant.
Arrange the sliced potatoes and carrots on the prepared baking sheet in a single layer.
Place the sea bass fillets on top of the potatoes and carrots, leaving a small gap between each fillet.
Brush the mixture from the bowl over the sea bass fillets, making sure to coat them evenly.
Transfer the baking sheet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving.
